907.name: Below Floor Level
907.description: You've managed to make your way down below the stone floor of the hall and level with the flowing magma. Several pieces of the shattered floor bob and float atop the molten river, though they don't look very safe. At least the rock you're standing on now is good and solid.

npcs.name: Xochlan
npcs.description: off its body as though it had just recently been aflame.

915.name: Breathless Cold
915.description: The freezing water rushes past you with intense speed. Only the handholds carved into the icy walls allow you to hold your position long enough to make it to the bottom. Occasionally a stark white, eyeless fish will pass you by, but other than that little could survive in these icy depths. It resists all attempts at picking the lock. Frosty Corridor The long ice filled hallway stretches out to the east, while to the north the unnatural chill dies off in a dark stone corridor. The floor below your feet is a thick layer of ice, but a man sized hole has been carved into it near one wall, with several handholds leading into the chilly water.

921.name: Trapped Under Ice
921.description: As the unstoppable current pushes you farther downstream under the ice escape becomes more and more of a priority. Using all your might you could probably make it back east to the hole you came in from, but any farther west and it would be impossible to make your way out alive. You'd best swim hard.

903.name: Trial By Fire
903.description: A long hallway stretches out before you, the far end obscured by shimmering waves of heat. The floor has fallen away in places to reveal a river of lava flowing lengthwise down the hall, directly below your feet. Fortunately what is left seems to form a fairly sturdy path west through the blistering heat.

npcs.name: Xiuhtlacon
npcs.description: dragon.  Charred wings scrape along the ground as it half drags and half

904.name: Precarious Footing
904.description: Here there is more empty air than there is foot room, which is very disconcerting given your proximity to a river of magma. The air shimmers with heat, and like many other places in the temple, the wall paintings seem to come alive in the haze. Blackened coal seams stain the surrounding rock.

905.name: End of the Line
905.description: Though the floor does actually continue, the twenty foot gap of open space makes forward progress treacherous at best. A thin ledge runs around the outside of the room, and several pieces of fallen floor still float on the surface of the river below you.

906.name: On the Ledge
906.description: You are kneeling upon the thin ledge running around the outer wall of the hallway. Much of it has crumbled away, but if one was careful they could safely make it across the large gap in the floor below to solid ground on the other side.

908.name: Hallway Bend
908.description: The hallway curves ninety degrees here, south into the relative shelter of a darkened corridor, while the floor drops off sharply to the east, into the searing torrent of lava. Well preserved wall paintings cover much of the red stone.

909.name: Darkened Hall
909.description: The corridor is pitch black, and choking with dust. A layer of silken threads has been spun over most of the walls and floor, creating a sort of funnel shape out of the rectangular hall. Several strands of the silk hang lose and stick fast to your clothes as you brush against them. They're more annoying than a real hindrance, but you wouldn't want to run into any of the walls.

npcs.name: Tchotxun spider
npcs.description: to the ground.  It is covered in a hard exoskeleton that resembles ice, and strange blue fire seeps from the cracks, flickering languidly.

910.name: Mess of Spider Webs
910.description: Silken strands line the hallway from floor to ceiling, clinging to your feet with every step and grabbing on to you whenever you happen too close to them. Hundreds of tiny arachnids scurry away from your lumbering presence. Several corpses have been wrapped up in the thread and drained of all their bodily fluids.

911.name: Spider Junction
911.description: This four way intersection in the stone corridor is carpeted with fresh webbing. High on the ceiling above you hang the bodies of several recent victims, cocooned tightly in spider silk. Slow acting venom keeps them somewhat alive while dissolving their insides.

npcs.name: Tchotlin spider
npcs.description: ball of flame.  The legs are white as bleached bone and appear brittle, but carry the spider along with unnerving speed and grace.

912.name: Cobwebs
912.description: This bend in the hallway is stuffed with cobwebs. Every visible surface is covered in a thick layer of sticky spider silk. Even the ground below your feet is coated with the soft but sticky strands, making walking much more of a chore than it should be, and sending vibrations along the whole complex system of webs with every step.

913.name: Sparkling Webs
913.description: The room is coated in a layer of sticky cobwebs. Frost has gathered on the strands and then melted, leaving behind a network of tiny water droplets that catch and refract any available light like miniature stars. Freezing air rolls in from a stone archway to the south.

914.name: Frosty Corridor
914.description: The long ice filled hallway stretches out to the east, while to the north the unnatural chill dies off in a dark stone corridor. The floor below your feet is a thick layer of ice, but a man sized hole has been carved into it near one wall, with several handholds leading into the chilly water.

npcs.name: Mictoachu
npcs.description: ice, sniffing at the minute changes in air temperature left by your passing. His fur is flawlessly white, save for the stains of blood around his muzzle

916.name: Hole in the Ice
916.description: The floor of ice above the river is much thinner here. Still enough to support your weight, but something large has smashed a hole through, and the water flowing out keeps the ice from thickening up too much. Several large icicles sit near the rim of the hole.

917.name: Thin Ice
917.description: The thick curtain of ice covering the river below thins out here, and in one spot the water bubbles to the surface, creating an impassable gap. Several large icicles rise from the ground, but none hang from the ceiling above. They appear to have fallen down, and probably are what made the large hole in the ice.

npcs.name: Living ice
npcs.description: time of creation.  It is not actually alive per se, but is a sentient being with a neural capacity similar to many invertebrates.

919.name: Icy River
919.description: You stand atop a thick slab of ice, the frozen top layer of an icy cold river. The unnatural chill seems localized to this one long hallway, and must be the work of some powerful enchantment. Giant icicles grow from both the floor and ceiling of the corridor.

npcs.name: Ice daemon
npcs.description: a roughly humanoid conglomeration.  Its lower body is comically small, with tiny hoofed feet that have less trouble holding it up than you would think.

920.name: Bottom of the Ice Wall
920.description: An almost vertical wall of ice rises high above your head. A small archway can be seen at its top, but it may as well not be there at all, since reaching it would be completely impossible. Below your feet, and a thick layer of ice, a frigid river flows by at tremendous speed.

922.name: Sacrificial Cells
922.description: A row of cells lines the north and south walls of this corridor, used to temporarily hold victims for sacrifice. The doors are made of thick metal and set deep into the stone walls. Why weakened prisoners awaiting their last days would need such stout barriers to hold them in seems a little strange to you, but then again, you've never captured anyone for sacrifice before have you?

923.name: Dead End Hall
923.description: More cells line the north and south walls here, where the corridor dead ends against another stone wall. Though free of any cell doors, it has evenly spaced indentations carved into it like a ladder, which lead up to the stone ceiling of the chamber.

924.name: Empty Cell
924.description: This cell is damp, smelly, and disgusting. Rats squeal, disturbed by your presence, and cease gnawing on the rotted corpse left on the floor. At least he never made it to the sacrificial altar.

925.name: Cell for the Days Sacrifice
925.description: This sparse square room was clearly not meant for extended occupation. There is no bed, no water bucket, no latrine hole, just four bare walls covered in disparate scratch marks and illegible scrawling.

npcs.name: Broken man
npcs.description: the brink of logic into a desparate rush at you, and the cell door.

926.name: Cell of the Moon
926.description: This cell is used to hold the annual sacrifice to the god of the moon. The walls are stained jet black, and a lunar calendar has been painted all around the room. The full moon symbol nearest the summer solstice is painted a deep red.

927.name: Cell of the Sun
927.description: This brightly lit cell holds the years sacrifice to the great sun god. Gold leaf covers the walls and ceiling, and the room glows strongly with magical light. Though certainly not dank and dingy like most dungeon cells you've been in, the intense light would make sleep near impossible and bring upon insanity in short order.

928.name: Top of a Stone Ladder
928.description: You stand in a small cubby in the southern wall of a dark hallway. At your feet is a hole in the ground leading down to the floor level of the red stone ziggurat. The musty smell of stale dust and old death assails your nostrils.

929.name: Stone Hallway
929.description: This dusty stone hallway runs east and west away from you, into darkness. Depictions of sacrifice and slaughter line the walls, and bloody hand prints are smeared along its surface. Erratic shuffling and groaning can be heard coming from both directions. Swallowing its fear, an ancient mummy swings at your head. (9) You thrust at an ancient mummy in vain.

npcs.name: Ancient mummy
npcs.description: over with each step.  Don't let its awkward movements fool you though, magical fire burns behind the glassy dead eyes.

npcs.name: Young serpent
npcs.description: Lucky for you this beast isn't full grown.  Still an adolescent, its scales havent quite hardened up yet, and while still fast, he doesnt have the same deadly speed of an adult.

930.name: Bend in the Corridor
930.description: The hall makes sharp bend here, presumably following the contours of the square, tiered walls of the ziggurat. The walls are made of near seamless stone, the cracks where the huge blocks are joined together are so tight one could not even slip a piece of paper between them.

931.name: Blood Stained Hall
931.description: The walls here are made of the same red stone as the rest of the pyramid but are stained much darker with semi-dried blood. Thick gooey puddles sit on the ground, almost congealed. With no visible source, you are forced to assume this is someones idea of decoration, or that the walls themselves are bleeding.

s.to_room_name: End of a Blood Stained Hall

932.name: End of a Blood Stained Hall
932.description: The hallway stops here before a stone slab recessed in the eastern wall. Dark blood drips from the visible gap between the slab and the sill, and is slowly seeping from the other miniscule joints in the stone. A single torch is placed in a sconce above the door, sending flickering shadows across the room.

933.name: Altar to the Moon
933.description: Several candles set on tall stands give the room a pungent aroma while bathing it in flickering light. Blood is everywhere; spattered on the candlesticks, large arcs sprayed onto the walls and ceiling, and of course a slow trickle out of the walls themselves. A black stone altar carved with lunar symbols fills most of the small shrine.

w.to_room_name: End of a Blood Stained Hall

npcs.name: Chantico
npcs.description: like the shadow of the moon.  Serrated fangs jut down from its upper lip and a slathering tongue holds a smaller mouth lined with a ring of teeth.

934.name: Dusty Corridor
934.description: The oppressive atmosphere of the low stone hallway is compounded by the thick pall of dust that fills the air. The ground is also covered in a layer of dust, and shows evidence of passing feet other than your own. You have lost 600 experience.

935.name: Burial Chamber
935.description: The hallway widens into a larger room here dominated by three raised stone slabs. Upon one of the slabs is the mummified corpse of someone who was apparently fairly important. His slab is in a position higher than the other two, which are curiously empty.

npcs.name: Bloody corpse
npcs.description: and kept his mortal body animated for evil purposes.  A gaping hole in his chest gives you a view of the wall behind him.

npcs.name: Wrath
npcs.description: while holding a still beating heart.  He shimmers and fades in and out of

936.name: T Junction
936.description: The hallway splits in three directions here. A heavy brass door is set into the wall to your west, while the hallway runs off to the south. Opposite the door is a dust covered wall painting. You have lost 1920 experience.

937.name: Altar to the Sun
937.description: A convex golden dome hangs from the ceiling of the chamber like the sun itself. It's lowest point comes less than a foot from the top of the altar. A victim would have to be forced into the tight space between the dome and the top of the altar, but given the situation you doubt comfort was a top priority.

npcs.name: Brass statue
npcs.description: no other human features.  The metal is absolutely seamless and moves like

938.name: Dark Room
938.description: This large room stretches out into the darkness before you. The complete silence is oppressive, and only compounds the feeling that the walls and ceiling are closing in on you. Every now and again a deep groan or a shuffling of feet will break the painful silence, though it is far from the reprieve one would hope for.

939.name: Within a Circle of Protection
939.description: A small camp is set up here, there is evidence of a small fire, and four bedrolls are still laid out on the ground in good condition. Various articles of survival equipment are strewn about the area, untouched by the horrid creatures roaming the halls. A large and very elaborate circle of protection is drawn onto the ground surrounding the area.

940.name: Battle Site
940.description: This corner of the large room was the site of a slaughter. There are no bodies, or even blood left behind, only the signs of a hard fought battle. The walls are pitted and charred from magical blasts, shattered suits of armor lay in decay, and all weapons have been mangled beyond any possible use, except as scrap metal.

941.name: Desecrated Burial
941.description: This corner of the large dark room is filled with several upturned sarcophagi, the contents of which have been hacked to pieces and put to the torch. Nothing of value appears in the ashen remains all is looted or destroyed by fire. The smell of burnt death on the air turns your stomach.

942.name: Bloody Hall
942.description: The floor here is covered in a slick carpet of congealed blood, footprints left in the gooey mess keep their shape for a time before being slowly filled by more of the red stuff seeping out of the walls. A basin jutting out from the north wall collects the blood and somehow keeps it permanently liquid.

943.name: Tight Cubby
943.description: The hallway closes in and dead ends here in a tiny cramped room. The seamless ceiling above your head is broken by a recessed slab of stone set in a circular depression. A set of grooves carved into the wall just below the hole forms a short ladder.

944.name: Top of the Ladder
944.description: This room is very different from the rest of the temple. The ceiling is high above your head, and instead of cramped, twisting hallways, the tier seems to be one large room. Vision is limited in the gloom, however and darkness obscures everything outside your immediate vicinity.

945.name: Stone Platform
945.description: It becomes blatantly obvious where the blood seeping from the walls in the tier below came from. This entire level seems to be a large reservoir of blood, set with stone platforms connected by sculpted bridges. It would be impossible to tell just how deep the pool was without having to jump into the viscous mess.

946.name: Crying Face
946.description: The wall directly to your north is covered with a huge carving of a sinister looking face. The man looks to be half elven, but has several strikingly daemonic features. A steady flow of blood runs out of each eye, into the giant pool.

947.name: Wide Stone Bridge
947.description: You stand in the center of a large, arcing bridge made of the same red stones as the walls. The bridge has no supporting posts, and no visible connecting mechanism between the blocks, all are held in place by the large keystone upon which you stand.

npcs.name: Humunaptun
npcs.description: is his hair and long snakelike lower body.

948.name: Cramped Room
948.description: This small room is separated from the larger room by thick walls of red stone. More space could have been achieved were the walls not over a meter thick, but as it is now there is barely enough room for a few people to stretch out. But at least theres no risk of falling into a pool of blood in here.

949.name: Swaying Bridge
949.description: This flimsy bridge is made of vines and planks of old wood. Though the gap it spans is fairly short, the drop into the pool is substantial, and would definitely be unpleasant. It feels sturdy enough, but dallying around would be ill advised.

950.name: Furnished Chamber
950.description: This room holds the only furniture youve seen in the whole place, and its still fairly spartan. A small cot looks very uncomfortable, and a chair is pushed up to a stone slab jutting out from the wall, serving as a desk. A small candle sputters and gives off a weak light.

npcs.name: Oahacu
npcs.description: crimson robes are accented with colorful beads and extensive jewelry and a bronze mask with a large sun crest covers his head and face.

951.name: Crumbling Columns
951.description: Several large columns have been toppled over on this stone platform, a few others are left in various stages of uprightness. The effect is a complex and difficult to navigate path through what is, in reality an open room. Bones lay strewn about the area.

npcs.name: Txuncoatl
npcs.description: columns,  supported by deceptively small wings.  You have little time to marvel at his vibrant plumage as he charges to the attack.

952.name: Base of a Stone Pillar
952.description: This huge redstone pillar rises to the ceiling and descends down into the pool of blood far below. A spiral staircase hugs the outside of the column and twists its way up into the darkness above.

953.name: Climbing the Pillar
953.description: The stairs are wide and flat, and though there is no railing to one side of you, the path is sturdy and you surely face more danger from heart failure as you climb the endless stairs than you do from falling off the edge.

898.name: Before an Immense Pyramid
898.description: Suddenly, like a great huge beast, an immense red structure rears out of the swamp. How you could have missed it before is a complete mystery, four tiers stretching up through the twisted trees. From afar you see huge feathered serpents carved into the walls, along with giant warrior and hunter motifs. Deadly quickmud surrounds much of the temples base, but a thin stretch of solid land connects at a point along the glyph covered wall.

899.name: Glyph Covered Wall
899.description: A thin isthmus of solid ground reaches out to the base of one of the deep red walls. A moat of quickmud surrounds the ziggurat on all other sides, barring access to the rest of the rune covered stone. Black glyphs flare up as you approach the wall.

901.name: Entrance Chamber
901.description: More of the strange writing lines the walls in this stuffy chamber. However, it does not hold the same angry glow as the runes outside. The room itself is split down the middle by a vein of black stone, the southern side is coated with a thin layer of frost, while the north emanates an uncomfortable heat. A brilliant orange flower thrives here, its petals flickering like flame.

npcs.name: Tenochlitxun
npcs.description: muscles, its body crouches with catlike posture.  But the head is misshapen with a huge oversized maw and a ridge of spines crowning its brow.

902.name: Before the Cleansing Fire
902.description: A roaring pillar of fire dominates one end of this faintly glowing hallway, throwing curling shadows across the room and making the wall paintings dance with life. A stone obelisk with a crying face sits dead center in the room.

918.name: Frigid Water
918.description: You land with a splash in the icy water and are sucked immediately under by the rushing river. The bottom of the sheet of ice above you if fairly rough, and handholds are many, but fighting the strong current is only possible for so long. With no way to climb back to onto the ice from here your only hope lay downstream.

954.name: The Weeper's Chamber
954.description: This room is a stark contrast to the dusty tomblike atmosphere of the rest of the ziggurat. The room is brightly lit, and decorated with elaborate tapestries and vivid paintings of lush plains and rivers. The ceiling is covered with a complete skymap, showing all the constellations as well as all the phases of the moon. Incense burns giving the room a pleasing aroma.

npcs.name: Weeper
npcs.description: something else entirely.  He is clean shaven and bald, and looks to be in his early twenties.  He has catlike eyes, and small fangs jut from his wry

955.name: The Heavens
955.description: The view of the Great Ancestral Swamp from the small balcony is breathtaking. Who would have thought the fetid marsh you cursed on the way through could hold such natural beauty? Mist hangs about in low areas, while the path of the Frektal can be followed by the steam rising up from its heated waters. The bulk of M'chel and the sskyyth's wondrous temple can be seen far in the distance.

npcs.name: Samsquanch
npcs.description: weeper to be his trusted bodyguard.  He doesnt say much, but you just know he would like nothing more than to pull your arms from their sockets.
